title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who spent time working on organic farms. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

content.title: Back to the Land: The Farm Volunteer

texts.A:
### Liam, The Vineyard in Tuscany
'I thought it would be all wine tastings, but it was back-breaking labor,' Liam says. [47]Initially, the sheer intensity of the sun was something I hadn't factored into my romanticized vision of Italy.[/47] [53]I learned that patience is essential; you can't rush the vines, you can only support their natural cycle.[/53] [51]The most difficult part was the early morning starts, which felt brutal after years of being a night owl.[/51] [48]I was surprised to find that my persistent insomnia disappeared after just a week of physical work and fresh air.[/48] [50]I realized that my background in engineering helped me fix a broken irrigation system that had been troubling the owners.[/50] It was a grounding experience.
texts.B:
### Sarah, The Sheep Farm in Scotland
Sarah volunteered during the lambing season. 'It was a lesson in resilience,' she explains. [47]I hadn't expected the emotional toll of the unpredictable weather on my motivation during the first few days.[/47] [54]What kept me going was the quiet dignity of the farmers, who worked tirelessly without a single complaint.[/54] [52]I hadn't anticipated the deep sense of satisfaction that comes from seeing a newborn lamb thrive under your care.[/52] [49]The experience taught me that we are far more disconnected from the sources of our food than we realize.[/49] [56]If I hadn't seen a flyer at my local library, I never would have known such programs existed.[/56] It was a life-altering month.
texts.C:
### Victor, The Olive Grove in Spain
Victor helped with the traditional harvest. 'It felt like stepping back in time,' he notes. [51]We struggled with the repetitive nature of the work, which required a mental stamina I didn't know I possessed.[/51] [48]I noticed that my general anxiety levels dropped significantly, likely due to the absence of digital notifications.[/48] [53]I realized that true quality in a product comes from a deep respect for the land and its history.[/53] [50]I found that the focus I used in my previous career as a researcher was perfect for cataloging different tree varieties.[/50] [55]It took a while to adjust to the slow pace of rural life, which felt frustratingly inefficient at first.[/55] [52]I’d initially worried it would be too lonely, but the communal lunches were vibrant.
texts.D:
### Elena, The Permaculture Project
Elena worked on an urban farm in Berlin. 'It’s about creating an ecosystem,' she says. [49]The project made me realize that sustainability isn't just a buzzword; it's a practical, daily commitment.[/49] [56]I wouldn't have found the farm if my sister hadn't recommended it after her own stay there.[/56] [54]I was moved by the diverse backgrounds of the volunteers, all united by a single green goal.[/54] [47]I hadn't expected the noise of the city to feel so intrusive while I was trying to connect with nature.[/47] [55]I found the initial learning curve of identifying 'weeds' versus 'crops' quite steep and confusing.[/55] [52]I’d been concerned that I wouldn't be strong enough, but the work was more about technique than muscle.

questions.question: mentions being surprised by how an external environmental factor affected their experience?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) mentions the sun, Sarah (B) the weather, and Elena (D) the city noise.

questions.question: notes a positive change in their physical or mental well-being?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) mentions his insomnia vanished, and Victor (C) mentions his anxiety levels dropped.

questions.question: expresses a change in their perception of a global or societal issue?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) reflects on food disconnection, and Elena (D) on the reality of sustainability.

questions.question: mentions how their previous professional skills or traits were beneficial?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) used his engineering skills, and Victor (C) used his research focus.

questions.question: describes a specific challenge related to their personal habits or mental endurance?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) struggled with being a night owl, and Victor (C) with the mental stamina for repetitive work.

questions.question: admits to having had prior concerns that were proven wrong by the experience?
questions.explanation: Victor (C) worried about loneliness, and Elena (D) worried about her physical strength.

questions.question: reflects on a deeper lesson learned about the nature of time or quality?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) learned about the natural cycle, and Victor (C) about respect for history and quality.

questions.question: highlights the value of the people or the social environment they encountered?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) mentions the farmers' dignity, and Elena (D) the diverse backgrounds of volunteers.

questions.question: describes a period of frustration while adapting to a new pace or knowledge set?
questions.explanation: Victor (C) found the slow pace frustrating, and Elena (D) found the initial learning curve confusing.

questions.question: acknowledges the specific way they became aware of the opportunity?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) saw a flyer in a library, and Elena (D) followed her sister's recommendation.
